feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: What men should know about the US government’s latest move on testosterone therapy
entry.summary: The US Department of Health and Human Services is requesting revisions to the labels on testosterone replacement therapies for men after reviewing new data and evidence on their safety and benefits. These updates could pave the way for easier access to testos…

entry.title: 4 Things to Avoid Every Day If You’re Watching Your Cholesterol
entry.summary: Nutrition experts share four behaviors that can impact cholesterol levels, suggesting alternative solutions to improve your cholesterol for the long haul.

entry.title: Popular weight-loss drugs linked to unexpected male fertility benefit
entry.summary: GLP-1 drugs like semaglutide and liraglutide do not appear to suppress male hormones and may boost fertility in obese men, new research suggests.

entry.title: Ozempic and Wegovy may strengthen bones in Type 2 diabetes patients, study finds
entry.summary: A new study suggest that semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy weight loss drugs, have an unexpected side effect of strengthening bones by 15%.

entry.title: 97 Amazing Facts About Our World To Keep You Busy For A Few Minutes
entry.summary: Tired of the endless political drama and the non-stop doomscrolling? It’s time for a break that actually leaves some positive vibes.

entry.title: Utah marks a year of battling measles, with no clear end in sight
entry.summary: Utah has spent the past year fighting measles outbreaks in almost every county. And experts say there's no clear end in sight. While some parts of the state have successfully stopped the spread, the highly contagious virus is continuing its steady march. The …

entry.title: Strange symptom leads to man's Stage IV cancer diagnosis
entry.summary: An app notification informed Joe Gillette that he had been diagnosed with aggressive, late-stage cancer.

entry.title: ‘Ghost fat’ is a surprising side effect of injectable weight-loss drugs
entry.summary: People experiencing "ghost fat" may instinctively continue behaviors they developed when they were larger.

entry.title: The truth about how chiggers bite your skin will actually horrify you
entry.summary: Two words guaranteed to destroy your summer: skin straw.

entry.title: You'd Never Guess This Subtle Sign Of A Stroke
entry.summary: Stroke symptoms aren't always so obvious. Here's how to spot one coming on.

entry.title: Woman went for routine surgery, ended up losing her arm–Worse was to come
entry.summary: Wioletta Wyrwol underwent routine surgery in 2013. A decade on, and more than 80 surgeries later, her health battles continue.

entry.title: Most TikTok sunscreen content is accurate. The misinformation gets the attention.
entry.summary: The critical videos included claims that sunscreen was toxic and contained carcinogens and that sun exposure is not dangerous.

entry.title: Why bodyweight exercises aren’t always better than lifting weights
entry.summary: Most strength-focused exercise falls into one of two broad categories: resistance training and bodyweight training. Here’s what each involves.

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: A fish stabs a man through the throat and the base of the skull
entry.summary: While on a fishing trip, a man sustained an unusual injury from a marlin.

entry.title: Woman Spends $54K on 'Life-Changing' Dental Implants, but Didn't Expect the 'Scary Looking' Aftermath (Exclusive)
entry.summary: “I just wanted to show people what it was like to lose your teeth at a young age and how I was coping with it,” Lacey Duvall tells PEOPLE

entry.title: Sugar-Free Diets May Have a Hidden Side Effect, Study in Mice Suggests
entry.summary: Cutting down on sugar delivers a host of health benefits, from minimizing tooth decay to improving mental health.

entry.title: First Suspected Ebola Case in Israel Transferred to Isolation, Health Ministry Says
entry.summary: According to the ministry's statement, the individual returned from the Democratic Republic of the Congo about three days ago and is experiencing symptoms such as fever and headache

entry.title: Australia pledges action on H5N1 after bird flu case confirmed
entry.summary: Australia confirmed its first mainland H5N1 bird flu case in a seabird near Esperance, WA.

entry.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
entry.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

entry.title: At least 30 dead in Congo camp as Ebola outbreak raises alarm
entry.summary: BUNIA, Democratic Republic of Congo >> At least 30 people have died since the start of May in one camp for displaced civilians in northeastern Congo, a death rate that camp officials said was unprecedented and, because of the symptoms, could indicate Ebola is…

entry.title: CDC says kissing bug disease is endemic in the U.S., where it can lurk in the heart for decades
entry.summary: As a result, infections can go undetected until severe heart damage has already begun.

entry.title: Popping a cocktail of supplements every day might be doing you more harm than good
entry.summary: While many of us take supplements regularly, there are some risks people may not be aware of.

entry.title: Measles-infected traveler may have exposed passengers at LAX and nearby hotel, health officials warn
entry.summary: A measles-infected traveler may have exposed passengers at LAX and a nearby Hilton hotel on June 11, marking the sixth case in LA County this year.

entry.title: Mourners bury a 6-month-old Ebola victim, marking third orphanage death as Congo outbreak spreads
entry.summary: Mourners in eastern Congo have gathered to bury a 6-month-old girl who died from Ebola earlier this week

entry.title: Research shows weight-loss drugs may also reduce cancer risk
entry.summary: A growing body of research suggests popular weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy may also help treat or prevent certain cancers. It’s the latest unexpected benefit to be associated with GLP-1s, which are now taken by one-in-eight American adults. William…

entry.title: Mosquito-borne virus detected in Central Florida as summer begins
entry.summary: Mosquito-borne virus detected in Central Florida as summer begins

entry.title: A new study rewrites the history of the plague
entry.summary: A new study looks at one of the most prolific pathogens in human history — the plague.

entry.title: Rare tick disease poses a danger in Minnesota lakes area
entry.summary: Researchers from the University of Minnesota estimate 5% of ticks in the state carry the Powassan virus, which has no treatment and can cause brain inflammation.

entry.title: Scientists Uncover Cause of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Solving Decades-Old Mystery
entry.summary: Scientists identified an autoimmune mechanism linked to a major IBD risk gene, opening the door to targeted diagnosis and personalized therapies for thousands of patients.

entry.title: New Trigger for Alzheimer’s Disease May Have Been Found
entry.summary: Evidence suggests the earliest changes may begin deep inside neurons, pointing researchers toward a new piece of the puzzle.

entry.title: More than 70 medics infected with Ebola as DRC outbreak spreads ‘fast’
entry.summary: Aid cuts and poor sanitation are deepening fears that Ebola is spreading through displacement camps.

entry.title: Scientists may have solved weight regain problem from GLP-1 drugs
entry.summary: The procedure stimulates regrowth of healthy tissue that is "reset" to the person's new, lower body weight.

entry.title: Michigan sees sharp rise in nitrous oxide use that lead to ER visits
entry.summary: Michiganders are inreasingly showing up in hospital ERs, calling 911 or the state poison center after using nitrous oxide. Here's why that matters.

entry.title: Feeling Tired After Lunch Is Common—Dietitians Share 3 Tips to Avoid Afternoon Fatigue
entry.summary: Feeling tired after lunch? You're not alone. Dietitians share 3 proven tips to prevent afternoon fatigue and maintain steady energy levels throughout the day.

entry.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda
entry.summary: The Bundibugyo virus disease (BVD) outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo continues to evolve rapidly, with sustained transmission and increasing numbers of reported cases. As of 17 June, a cumulative of 896 confirmed cases, including 232 deaths, ha…

entry.title: Science finds why people with depression feel so stuck – their brain may be 'entrapping' them
entry.summary: Brain Entrapment Study: Discover how depression affects brain patterns, leading to cognitive rigidity. Mount Sinai's research unveils why transitioning between mental states is difficult in depression, causing a sense of being 'stuck'.

entry.title: Trump admin seeks to roll back testosterone restrictions — why docs are conflicted
entry.summary: The 2015 warnings were added because concerns about heart risks were raised, plus evidence of benefits were “limited” at the time, the HHS said. The department said research has come ou…

entry.title: Non-surgical procedure may help relieve knee pain
entry.summary: Osteoarthritis, the most common form of arthritis, causes inflammation, stiffness, reduced mobility and sensory nerve pain.

entry.title: A yoga therapist says tension in your jaw and sacrum can be linked—this routine will soothe both
entry.summary: If you find yourself clenching your jaw, this gentle routine could help

entry.title: Guest Column | Facing cancer, I now understand the hidden lesson in my father’s final days
entry.summary: As a Holocaust survivor, my father knew about adversity and survival. But I'm learning something else from the end of his life.

entry.title: Southern Utah health expert says this common habit is as harmful as smoking
entry.summary: To this day, the No. 1 cause of death in the United States of America is reportedly heart disease. While it can be unavoidable in certain circumstances, residents can take

entry.title: 4 Myths about Alzheimer’s You Need to Stop Believing
entry.summary: A neurologist breaks down 4 common myths about Alzheimer’s disease, including who gets it, what causes it and how it may be treated.

entry.title: The Most Promising Ebola Vaccine Has Been Sitting on the Shelf for 15 Years
entry.summary: Years after initial tests, researchers are now racing to see if a vaccine developed in 2011 can help fight the current Bundibugyo outbreak in Congo.

entry.title: Little-known tick bite disease leaves man nonverbal — what to know about the rising threat
entry.summary: According to friends and family, John Reagan, 66, is nonverbal after he was bitten by a tick while walking his dog.

entry.title: ‘Ghost fat’ is a surprising side effect of injectable weight-loss drugs
entry.summary: People experiencing "ghost fat" may instinctively continue behaviors they developed when they were larger.

entry.title: Ebola in DR Congo: Six-year-old 'doing well' after armed men took her from hospital
entry.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

entry.title: A 50-year-old PT shares the 4 simple exercises you need to start strength training later in life – here's how to do them
entry.summary: A 50-year-old PT shows you how to build menopausal strength with two dumbbells and just four simple movement patterns – the squat, hinge, push and pull.

entry.title: ‘Ghost fat’ is a surprising side effect of injectable weight-loss drugs
entry.summary: People experiencing "ghost fat" may instinctively continue behaviors they developed when they were larger.

entry.title: Doctors Say These Are the Top 5 Foods That Cause Inflammation
entry.summary: A doctor and dietitian reveal the top foods that cause inflammation to limit or avoid, plus the key features of an anti-inflammatory diet.

entry.title: The #3 Cause of Cancer Deaths Is Rising Fast, But 60% of Cases May Be Preventable
entry.summary: Liver cancer is one of the fastest rising causes of cancer-related deaths in the UK, and the third leading cause of cancer deaths worldwide.
